title: Add std::process class
body:
This change contains initial support of std::process class, covering functionalities used by UVM.

The unsupported parts are:
1. .suspend() -- not used by UVM; handling it will require modifying each scheduler as suspension works differently for each of them,
2. .get_randstate()/.set_randstate() -- the change contains a partial support that assumes only one state variable is stored at a time; for full support we need a way to access and modify internal state of the RNG.

The implementation adds a new runtime VlProcess class for keeping track of process metadata.
The metadata is updated by schedulers and the VlCoroutineHandler type.
If some processes use metadata of a process, its termination shouldn't result in freeing the VlProcess object, so during verilation pointers to VlProcess are replaced with dedicated VlProcessRef (std::shared_ptr<VlProcess>).
Process control can be done using methods of VlProcess, and std::process is implemented as a Verilog interface for these methods through $c calls.
